sources.name: DeFiLlama Hacks
sources.license_or_rights: Public API. Treat as source metadata for derived summaries; do not resell raw API dumps.
sources.output_policy: Normalize incident metadata, hashes, links, and aggregate counts for defensive analysis.
sources.caveats: Some records have empty source links or missing amounts. Live API order can change.

sources.name: Chainalysis Blog RSS
sources.license_or_rights: Public RSS feed and article links. Use only metadata, short titles, categories, and links.
sources.output_policy: Surface linked research items and crime/security categories as source leads.
sources.caveats: Article body rights remain with Chainalysis; summaries should cite links rather than copy content.

sources.name: Forta GraphQL Alerts and Labels API
sources.license_or_rights: Forta API and premium-feed terms apply. Use alert hashes, labels, source ids, severity, chain ids, and redacted addresses only.
sources.output_policy: Use as digest-only exploit-stage intelligence; promote to wallet alerts only with direct wallet/source evidence.
sources.caveats: Disabled until API access, premium terms, and retention rules are reviewed.

sources.name: Forta Labelled Datasets
sources.license_or_rights: MIT repository. Underlying labels may derive from third-party sources; keep attribution and source hashes.
sources.output_policy: Use as labelled malicious-address and contract seed data for defensive detector tests.
sources.caveats: Treat labels as signals, not final truth; preserve source and confidence.

sources.name: CryptoScamDB
sources.license_or_rights: MIT repository. Use attribution and derived risk summaries.
sources.output_policy: Use as malicious URL/address provenance for agent domain and recipient checks.
sources.caveats: User-report quality varies; combine with other evidence.

sources.name: Scam Sniffer Scam Database
sources.license_or_rights: GPL-3.0 repository with delayed open data. Keep license boundaries explicit before bundling.
sources.output_policy: Use as optional phishing-domain signal with attribution, not raw resale data.
sources.caveats: Disabled by default to avoid accidental license mixing in closed artifacts.

sources.name: MetaMask eth-phishing-detect
sources.license_or_rights: Nonstandard DBAD-style license. Attribute and review before redistribution.
sources.output_policy: Use as optional phishing-domain comparison source, returning derived verdicts only.
sources.caveats: Disabled by default until redistribution posture is reviewed.

sources.name: Rekt News
sources.license_or_rights: Public site. Use link/provenance metadata and original-source citations; do not mirror article text.
sources.output_policy: Use as incident provenance lead and manual source URL for records.
sources.caveats: No stable public JSON feed is assumed by 0guard.

sources.name: SlowMist Hacked Archive
sources.license_or_rights: Public incident archive. Use source links, categories, and derived defensive analysis.
sources.output_policy: Use as provenance and enrichment source for incident records.
sources.caveats: Automated scraping is intentionally limited to availability checks until terms/API are explicit.

sources.name: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
sources.license_or_rights: U.S. government public data. Useful for operational exploit context, not wallet-specific verdicts.
sources.output_policy: Use only as auxiliary context for infrastructure exposure narratives.
sources.caveats: Not specific to DeFi or 0G; keep separate from transaction-level policy decisions.

sources.name: OFAC Sanctions List Service
sources.license_or_rights: U.S. government public sanctions data. Screen defensively; avoid private-person dossiering.
sources.output_policy: Use as an optional compliance enrichment source for sanctioned-entity links.
sources.caveats: Do not present sanctions context as legal advice.

sources.name: URLhaus
sources.license_or_rights: Free fair-use API; commercial or high-volume use may require a paid Spamhaus route.
sources.output_policy: Use as optional malware-infrastructure context, not as a Web3 phishing substitute.
sources.caveats: Malware URL focus; not enough alone for crypto transaction verdicts.

sources.name: Chainabuse Reports
sources.license_or_rights: API and platform terms apply. Use only with operator-provided credentials and permitted scopes.
sources.output_policy: Use as optional malicious-address/report enrichment, returning derived risk flags and links only.
sources.caveats: Disabled by default because it requires credentials and terms review.

sources.name: GoPlus Security APIs
sources.license_or_rights: API terms apply. Suitable as an upstream signal, not as raw resale data.
sources.output_policy: Use for transaction/token/address risk enrichment after explicit integration review.
sources.caveats: Keep disabled until API key/terms/rate limits are reviewed.

sources.name: BlockSec Security Blog
sources.license_or_rights: Public security blog. Use links, categories, short metadata, hashes, and defensive analysis; do not mirror full article text.
sources.output_policy: Use as reviewed incident root-cause provenance and detector-enrichment leads.
sources.caveats: Article body rights remain with BlockSec; manually promote only source-cited summaries and links.

sources.name: Tenderly Simulation API
sources.license_or_rights: Vendor API terms apply. Use state-delta summaries and links only unless terms allow trace storage.
sources.output_policy: Use as optional EVM transaction simulation enrichment for approvals, swaps, and contract calls.
sources.caveats: Disabled by default until credentials, limits, and trace-retention terms are reviewed.

sources.name: BlockSec Phalcon Simulation and Explorer
sources.license_or_rights: Vendor terms apply. Public incident/explorer links can be cited; raw traces should not be resold.
sources.output_policy: Use as optional transaction simulation and incident-context enrichment.
sources.caveats: Keep separate from public blog metadata and enable only after API terms are confirmed.

sources.name: TON Center API v3
sources.license_or_rights: Infrastructure API terms apply. Use derived activity features, source ids, and hashes only.
sources.output_policy: Use for read-only TON account, transaction, Jetton, and NFT activity enrichment.
sources.caveats: TON risk passport remains syntax/policy-only until a live indexer adapter is explicitly enabled.

sources.name: TONAPI Jetton and Account Activity APIs
sources.license_or_rights: TONAPI terms apply. Use derived account, Jetton, NFT, and transaction activity features only.
sources.output_policy: Use for read-only Telegram/TON risk passport enrichment without returning raw indexer dumps.
sources.caveats: No tonProof, wallet prompt, transaction send, or Telegram send is triggered by this adapter.

sources.name: Helius Enhanced Transactions and Webhooks
sources.license_or_rights: Vendor API terms apply. Use parsed, derived risk features and source links only.
sources.output_policy: Use for read-only Solana account/SPL-token risk monitoring and alert-quality gates.
sources.caveats: No Solana transaction signing or submission is in scope.

sources.name: LayerZero Scan API
sources.license_or_rights: Public API terms apply. Use derived message-status risk and links only.
sources.output_policy: Use for read-only message status, DVN/config, and stuck-message risk context.
sources.caveats: Never call send/receive or configuration transactions from this adapter.

sources.name: Wormholescan API
sources.license_or_rights: Public API terms apply. Use derived transfer/message metadata and links only.
sources.output_policy: Use for read-only VAA and transfer status context in Wormhole guardrails.
sources.caveats: No relayer call, VAA submission, or token transfer is executed.

sources.name: Hyperliquid Info and WebSocket APIs
sources.license_or_rights: Official API terms apply. Treat outputs as market/exposure context, not advice or execution.
sources.output_policy: Use for read-only exposure, fill, and suspicious activity context; never place or cancel orders.
sources.caveats: Trading, transfer, and withdrawal endpoints are out of scope.

sources.name: Multi-Chain Analytics APIs
sources.license_or_rights: Paid data terms vary. Build derived features, not raw query resale.
sources.output_policy: Use for nightly behavior features such as fan-out, mixer proximity, and new-contract exposure.
sources.caveats: Buy only when native adapters cannot cover needed chains at acceptable quality.

sources.name: Smart Contract Hacking Attack Library
sources.license_or_rights: Public attack-library pages. Use classification metadata, source links, hashes, and defensive analysis only.
sources.output_policy: Use as incident taxonomy and source-link provenance leads for defensive detector tests.
sources.caveats: Some entries are classification signals rather than protocol-authored postmortems; preserve caveats.

sources.name: MEXC News / LiveBitcoinNews Crypto Incident Reports
sources.license_or_rights: Public news pages. Use as corroborating metadata links only; do not mirror article text or treat market commentary as advice.
sources.output_policy: Use as secondary incident corroboration when protocol/security-firm postmortems are unavailable.
sources.caveats: News syndication can repeat unverified claims; require stronger sources before high-confidence detector promotion.
